Re-measured via real browser on #/issues Threads tab (scenario-staled by the route-rename yatsu.md edit; no forge configured, so LOCAL store only). 13 non-concluded rows in API order (newest first, no re-sort), each a compact line: LOCAL store chip · concern · status badge · reply count. Concluded hidden behind a '12 closed' chip; open/total meta ('13 open · 25 total') sits at the END of the bar; the store filter is absent (single store). Selecting a local thread opens it in the RIGHT detail: full header (open status, author, +0 signed, clickable node chip), body MARKDOWN-RENDERED (inline code spans render — no raw backticks/##/| pipes visible; 4 rendered block elements on the piped-stdout thread), replies, and a reply composer. 0 console errors.

Re-measured via real browser on #/issues (scenario-staled by the route-rename yatsu.md edit; mentions.jsx byte-unchanged). Selected a LOCAL issue: reply composer carries the console's shared mention menu (mentions.jsx). Typed '@' -> menu opens UPWARD (up class, rect above the docked textarea), head '// sessions — ↑↓ pick · ↵ insert', lists '@new' (no live sessions in the isolated clone, so sessions list is empty — @new only); ArrowDown+Enter inserts '@new ' (trailing space). Typed '[[side' -> node menu lists 'side-nav'; pick inserts '[[side-nav]] '. Esc closes the menu, KEEPS the draft ('@'), stays on #/issues. Plain prose opens NO menu. New-form body textarea '@' -> menu opens DOWNWARD (no up class, rect below the textarea). Send deliberately NOT fired (no thread write; measured in an isolated clone regardless). Console @/[[ menus not re-driven this run (no live session in the clone) but the shared mentions.jsx module is byte-identical and was exercised live in BOTH dashboard composers here, so the shared-module behavior is transitively confirmed. 0 console errors.

Re-measured via real browser on #/issues (scenario-staled by the route-rename yatsu.md edit; code byte-unchanged). List renders instantly from app-resident issues (no per-mount fetch — 0 board bursts on mount). j/k move ONE selection within the ACTIVE tab and the detail follows immediately (selection IS detail). Typing 'jkjk typed into input' into the New-form input reaches the input and never moves the selection. 0 console errors.
